# Skill: Bilingual Lesson Writing

Use this skill for English and Hindi lesson HTML files.

## Style

- Crash-course, last-minute revision tone.
- Short sections, bullets, tables, and classroom examples.
- Clear explanations for D.El.Ed students.
- Practical elementary-school classroom connections.
- Original writing only; no large copied passages.

## HTML Rules

- Write content snippets only.
- Do not include `<!doctype>`, `<html>`, `<head>`, or `<body>`.
- Use semantic tags: `h1`, `h2`, `h3`, `p`, `ul`, `ol`, `table`, `details`, `summary`.
- Use simple ASCII diagrams inside `pre` or code-style blocks only when useful.
- Keep media references relative to the language folder, usually `../media/file-name.png`.

## Parallel Coverage

- Create both English and Hindi files for every completed topic.
- Keep heading order mostly parallel.
- Keep examples culturally and classroom appropriate.
- Hindi should be natural and readable, not word-by-word translation.

## Suggested Lesson Pattern

```html
<h1>Topic Title</h1>

<p>One short purpose line.</p>

<h2>Exam Snapshot</h2>
<table>...</table>

<h2>Key Points</h2>
<ul>...</ul>

<h2>Classroom Connection</h2>
<p>...</p>

<h2>One-minute Revision</h2>
<ul>...</ul>

<h2>Common Exam Lines</h2>
<ul>...</ul>
```
